candidates.title: Encoder-Driven Feature Absorption: Mechanism and Safety Implications

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption. arXiv:2409.14507
collisions.overlap: Documents the absorption phenomenon in SAEs; assumes decoder geometry drives absorption
collisions.differentiation: Our H_Mech factorial directly refutes decoder assumption by showing decoder contributes nothing (Condition C ≈ A = 0.299, Condition B ≈ D = 0.484)

collisions.paper: Tang et al., 2025. Theoretical Foundation of SDL in MI. arXiv:2512.05534
collisions.overlap: Provides theoretical grounding for encoder-driven local minima in SAEs
collisions.differentiation: Tang provides theory; we provide experimental factorial validation + safety implications

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2026. Sanity Checks for SAEs. arXiv:2602.14111
collisions.differentiation: Their comparison is across SAEs; ours is within-SAE encoder vs decoder decomposition

collisions.paper: Basu et al., 2026. Interpretability without Actionability. arXiv:2603.18353
collisions.differentiation: Basu raises the concern; we provide the methodology to test whether absorption is the mechanism for safety feature failure

collisions.paper: Hu et al., 2025. Measuring SAE Feature Sensitivity. arXiv:2509.23717
collisions.differentiation: We combine sensitivity with absorption analysis; their work is standalone measurement

candidates.differentiation_notes: The 2x2 factorial decomposition (encoder vs decoder contribution) is genuinely novel methodology. No prior work has decomposed absorption this way. The decoder-irrelevance finding is a genuine correction to prior assumptions.

candidates.title: Safety-Critical Features Are Disproportionately Absorbed

collisions.paper: Basu et al., 2026. Interpretability without Actionability. arXiv:2603.18353
collisions.overlap: Raises concern that interpretability tools may fail for safety-critical cases
collisions.differentiation: Basu argues the problem exists theoretically; we empirically test whether absorption specifically is the mechanism causing safety feature failure
General safety feature context; no specific absorption claims

candidates.differentiation_notes: Genuinely novel: No prior work empirically tests whether safety-critical features are disproportionately absorbed in SAEs. The combination of (1) Neuronpedia-annotated safety features, (2) multi-child proportional absorption measurement, and (3) Mann-Whitney U test comparing safety vs non-safety is unique.

candidates.title: Encoder Regularization to Reduce Absorption

collisions.overlap: Orthogonal SAE training to reduce absorption by modifying both encoder and decoder
collisions.differentiation: OrtSAE modifies both encoder and decoder; our approach is encoder-only modification, which is a cleaner intervention if H_Mech is correct (encoder drives absorption)
Tang provides theory; we provide constructive intervention

candidates.differentiation_notes: Must clearly differentiate from OrtSAE by focusing on encoder-only intervention (not both). Emphasize theoretical basis from H_Mech (encoder drives absorption). Target <5% reconstruction degradation threshold.

candidates.title: Encoder Geometry Diagnostic for Training-Free Absorption Prediction

collisions.overlap: Provides theoretical framework for encoder direction geometry and absorption
collisions.differentiation: Our diagnostic would be an applied version of their theory; theoretical ground is already covered
General framework, not specific to absorption

candidates.differentiation_notes: Novelty is low because Tang's paper covers the theoretical basis. DEPRIORITIZED in proposal (original was about decoder geometry, but H_Mech shows decoder contributes nothing). Would need to operate on encoder directions instead. Score 4/10 is justified.

candidates.title: Multi-Resolution SAE Ensemble for Hierarchical Feature Recovery

collisions.differentiation: L0 diversity mechanism (16, 64, 256) is the claimed differentiator, but the core ensemble concept overlaps with Gadgil

candidates.differentiation_notes: Must emphasize the specific L0 diversity mechanism and compare explicitly against Gadgil's approach in methodology. Focus on hierarchical feature recovery aspect which Gadgil may not address.

candidates.title: Efficient Coding Framing (Replaces Competitive Exclusion)

collisions.paper: Barlow, H., 1961. Possible Principles Governing the Relearning of Sensory Data
collisions.differentiation: Cannot claim efficient coding as novelty; it's classical work from 1961

collisions.overlap: Supports efficient coding interpretation for absorption
collisions.differentiation: Supports but doesn't originate the concept

candidates.differentiation_notes: This is a theoretical reframing grounded in classical work. Proposal correctly acknowledges: 'Reinterpretation of mechanism, not new empirical contribution.' Novelty 2/10 is generous. Should be used as framing device within cand_p1 if at all.

key_collisions.chanin_2024: partial_overlap - absorption phenomenon documented, assumes decoder drives absorption (we refute)
key_collisions.tang_2512.05534: related_work - theoretical foundation for encoder geometry, does not provide experimental validation or safety analysis
key_collisions.korznikov_2025_ortsae: partial_overlap - modifies both encoder+decoder, we target encoder only
key_collisions.basu_2603.18353: related_work - raises safety concern, we test absorption as mechanism
key_collisions.gadgil_2025: partial_overlap - general ensemble concept, L0 diversity is differentiator
key_collisions.barlow_1961: exact_match (classical) - cannot claim efficient coding as novel

anti_patterns_avoided: Did not rubber-stamp novelty without searching - each claim checked against prior work
Did not dismiss ideas due to vaguely related work - precise overlap classification
Did not conflate related_work with already_done - same area ≠ collision
Did not skip candidates - all 6 assessed
